# Vagal-driven microglial priming

**Hypothesis.** Chronic LPS translocation from gut-derived bacteria primes microglia via subdiaphragmatic vagal afferents.

## Mechanism
LPS binds TLR4 on enteric afferents; signal traverses NTS → locus coeruleus → noradrenergic microglial activation.

## Entities
- TLR4
- subdiaphragmatic vagal nerve
- microglia

## Anticipated outcomes
Vagotomy abolishes microglial priming in LPS-fed mice.

## Novelty
Prior work assumed cytokine-mediated transfer; this proposes a direct neural route.

## Citations
- Doe et al 2024 — https://example.com/paper1
